revision.text:
[[File:Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg|right|thumb|300px|Shizuhatayama kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳|}} is a circular ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. It is protected by the central government as a national historic site.
Located on the hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine, the burial mound has a horizontal chamber of cut stones, surmounted by a stone dome, approximately seven meters in diameter. The interior burial chamber has a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearvy [[Abe River]] were used in its construction.
The burial chamber contains a stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]].
The ''kofun'' was [[Excavation (archaeology)|excavated]] in 1949. Numerous shards of [[Hagi ware]] and [[Sue ware]] were found on the floor of the tomb, and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person bured in the tomb is unknown. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at a small museum within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.
==See also== *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, ISBN 4-642-08979-9
==External links== *[http://www.city.shizuoka.jp/deps/bunkazai/bunkazai_tyousa_shizuhatakohun_index.htm Shizuoka City site] {{coord|35|00|40|N|139|26|24|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=title}}
[[Category:Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun]]
[[ja:賤機山古墳]]

[[File:Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg|right|thumb|300px|Shizuhatayama kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳|}} is a circular ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. It is protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].
Located on the hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine, the burial mound has a horizontal chamber of cut stones, surmounted by a stone dome, approximately seven meters in diameter. The interior burial chamber has a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction.
The burial chamber contains a stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]].
The ''kofun'' was [[Excavation (archaeology)|excavated]] in 1949. Numerous shards of [[Hagi ware]] and [[Sue ware]] were found on the floor of the tomb, and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at a small museum within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.
==See also== *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, ISBN 4-642-08979-9
==External links== *[http://www.city.shizuoka.jp/deps/bunkazai/bunkazai_tyousa_shizuhatakohun_index.htm Shizuoka City site] {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=title}}
[[Category:Buildings and structures in Shizuoka, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]

It is protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].
Located on the hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine, the burial mound has a horizontal chamber of cut stones, surmounted by a stone dome, approximately seven meters in diameter. The interior burial chamber has a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction.
The burial chamber contains a stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]].
The ''kofun'' was [[Excavation (archaeology)|excavated]] in 1949. Numerous shards of [[Hagi ware]] and [[Sue ware]] were found on the floor of the tomb, and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at a small museum within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.
==See also== *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = Kofun | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = National Historic Site | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a dome-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinta complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern survey was conducted in 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction.
The burial chamber contains a stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Hagi ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at a small museum within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction.
The burial chamber contains a stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Hagi ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at a small museum within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a dome-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Hagi ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]

The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]

Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]

The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sizuhatayama Kohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|58|58.3|N|138|22|29.9|E|region:JP_type:edu|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG|right|270px|thumb|exterior]] {{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in [[Aoi-ku, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]]. in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and [[Edo period]] documents indicate that the [[burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the [[Tenmei]] or [[Bunsei]] era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby [[Abe River]] were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone [[sarcophagus]] made of [[tuff]] from [[Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of [[Haji ware]] and [[Sue ware]], and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}
