{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shizuhatayama Kofun | native_name = 賤機山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shizuhatayama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Interior of the Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shizuhatayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.98286111|N|138.37497222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Aoi-ku|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Shizuhatayama Kofungun|WD=Q100331862|1=Shizuhatayama Kofungun}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum at site) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in {{ill|Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|en|Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|WD=Q1131483|lt=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} documents indicate that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} or {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby {{ill|Abe River|en|Abe River|WD=Q318136|lt=Abe River|1=Abe River}} were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} from [[:en:Izu peninsula|Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or Rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www.city.shizuoka.jp/000_002439.html Shizuoka City site] *[http://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/bunka/bk-180/b-walk/shizuoka04.html Shizuoka Prefecture official site]

{{nihongo|'''Shizuhatayama Kofun'''|賤機山古墳}} is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located within the grounds of the [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] complex in {{ill|Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|en|Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|WD=Q1131483|lt=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1953.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138657 |title=賤機山古墳 |trans-title= Shizuhatayama kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shizuhatayama Kofun is located on a 50-meter hilltop immediately behind the ''[[Honden]]'' of the Ohtoshimioya Shrine within the Shizuoka Sengen Jinja complex. Its existence has been known since ancient times and {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} documents indicate that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was exposed after a tree fell in a storm during the {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} or {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} era. The first modern [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1949 and 1955, at which time the structure of tumulus was found to consist of a stone dome, approximately 32 meters in diameter and seven meters in height. There was no evidence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. Within is a horizontal chamber of cut stones with a length of 6.5 meters, height of 2.3 meters and width of 1.8 meters. A rock-lined tunnel 18.2 meters long connects the burial chamber to an opening in the hillside, orientated to the south. Some 600 head-sized boulders from the nearby {{ill|Abe River|en|Abe River|WD=Q318136|lt=Abe River|1=Abe River}} were used in its construction. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber contains a house-shaped 2.9 meter stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} from [[:en:Izu peninsula|Izu peninsula]]. The lid had eight protrusions for hanging ropes, three on each side and one each on the head and foot. Although pillaged in pre-modern times, the burial chamber was found to contain numerous shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and remnants of armor, swords and bronze horse fittings were found within the sarcophagus, indicating that the burial dates from around the 6th century AD. The name or Rank of the person buried in the tomb is unknown and no human remains were discovered. The site came under government protection in 1953, and many of the artifacts uncovered are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Shizuoka City Cultural Property Museum'''|静岡市文化財資料館|Shizuoka-shi bunkazai shiryōkan}} within the grounds of the Shizuoka Sengen Shrine.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}} *[[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]]
==References== {{reflist}} *Goto, Ken’Ichi. ''The Breakup and Unification of Regional Distribution, as Seen in Sue Ware Recovered from Kofun: An Example using Sue from Sixth and Seventh Century Tombs of Western Suruga''. NIHON KÔKOGAKU. No. 9, May 2000, 149p; ISSN 1340-8488, {{ISBN|4-642-08979-9}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www.city.shizuoka.jp/000_002439.html Shizuoka City site] *[http://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/bunka/bk-180/b-walk/shizuoka04.html Shizuoka Prefecture official site]
